title: Add thread-safe Singleton variant alongside basic implementation
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Singleton only shows the basic (non-thread-safe) variant. In production and interviews, thread-safe Singleton (double-checked locking, enum singleton in Java, module-level in Python) is what matters. Add a second code tab showing the thread-safe variant for both TS and Python. CLRS and Sedgewick both emphasize this distinction.
acceptanceCriteria: Singleton pattern has two code variants: 'Basic' and 'Thread-Safe'
Thread-safe variant shows double-checked locking pattern in TypeScript
Thread-safe variant shows module-level approach in Python
Properties panel shows toggle between variants
Description explains WHEN thread-safety matters and WHEN it doesn't

title: Add undo/redo Command variant with concrete undo implementation
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Command pattern shows basic command execution but doesn't demonstrate the undo/redo capability — which is the primary reason Command exists. Add an undo variant showing: execute(), undo(), history stack. Show TextEditor example with insert/delete commands that can be undone.
acceptanceCriteria: Command pattern has 'With Undo' code variant for both TS and Python
Shows CommandHistory stack with execute() and undo() methods
TextEditor example: InsertCommand and DeleteCommand with undo
UML diagram variant shows the history/undo relationships

title: Add code samples to all 5 SOLID demos (currently UML only)
description: MULTI-REPRESENTATION GAP: SOLID demos have before/after UML diagrams and text explanations but NO code samples. Patterns have both UML AND code. SOLID should too. Add TS and Python code for both 'before' (violation) and 'after' (refactored) states of each SOLID principle. Example: SRP before = one UserManager class with 6 methods, after = 3 separate service classes.
acceptanceCriteria: SOLIDDemo type extended with beforeCode: { typescript: string; python: string } and afterCode fields
All 5 SOLID demos have before AND after code in both TS and Python
Code panel shows the active code (before or after) matching the current UML view
Code toggles when user switches between before/after UML view
Code is under 30 lines each for readability

title: Add educational inline comments to all 15 pattern code samples
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I10: All 15 patterns score 1/3 on documentation — only '// Usage' comments exist. Add 2-4 educational inline comments per code sample explaining WHY each design decision was made. Example for Observer: '// We store observers in a Set, not Array, because: (1) O(1) add/remove, (2) prevents duplicate subscriptions'. Comments should form a narrative — reading JUST the comments should teach the pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: All 15 patterns have 2-4 educational inline comments in TS code sample
All 15 patterns have 2-4 educational inline comments in Python code sample
Comments explain WHY (design decisions), not WHAT (code restating)
Comments address common questions: 'Why Set not Array?', 'Why private constructor?'
Reading only the comments gives a coherent explanation of the pattern

title: Add Observer push vs pull variant and EventEmitter variant
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Observer only shows basic push model. Missing: (1) Pull model where observers query the subject for specific data, (2) EventEmitter/event-bus variant common in Node.js/browser. These are the two most-asked Observer variants in interviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Observer pattern has 3 code variants: 'Push', 'Pull', 'EventEmitter'
Pull variant shows observers calling subject.getState() instead of receiving data
EventEmitter variant shows typed events with on/emit/off API
Each variant has both TS and Python code
Description explains when to use each variant

title: Add fluent Builder and lambda Strategy modern variants
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Builder shows Director-based GoF variant but modern code overwhelmingly uses fluent builder (method chaining with return this). Strategy shows interface-based variant but modern TS/Python uses lambdas/closures. Add modern variants for both. Builder: UserBuilder().name('Alice').email('a@b.com').build(). Strategy: pass function directly instead of creating a class.
acceptanceCriteria: Builder has 'Fluent Builder' variant with method chaining in TS and Python
Strategy has 'Lambda/Closure' variant showing function-based strategies
Both variants have code in TypeScript AND Python
Description explains: 'GoF version for understanding, modern version for production'

title: Add Proxy type variants — Protection, Caching, and Remote proxy examples
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Proxy shows only Virtual Proxy (lazy init). Interview question 'name 3 types of proxy' requires: Protection Proxy (access control), Caching Proxy (memoization), Remote Proxy (network). Add 2 more variant code tabs showing Protection and Caching proxies. Source: refactoring.guru lists 4 proxy types.
acceptanceCriteria: Proxy pattern has 3 code variants: 'Virtual', 'Protection', 'Caching'
Protection variant shows access control (user role check before operation)
Caching variant shows memoization (cache result of expensive operation)
Each variant has TS and Python code
Description explains: 'All proxies control access — they differ in WHY'

title: Add Iterator generator/yield variant for modern code style
description: IMPLEMENTATION AUDIT I8: Iterator shows manual hasNext()/next() interface. Modern Python uses yield/generators, modern TS uses Symbol.iterator/for-of. The manual iterator is educational but students should see the modern equivalent. Add 'Generator' variant showing Python yield and TS Symbol.iterator implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Iterator pattern has 'Manual' and 'Generator' code variants
Generator variant uses Python yield keyword
Generator variant uses TypeScript Symbol.iterator / for-of protocol
Description explains: 'Generators are syntactic sugar for the Iterator pattern'
